title: Click the Duck
author: system
description: A high-stakes mini-game where you must click a teleporting duck to survive the boredom.

title: Existential Dread
author: system
description: A minimalist space to contemplate the vastness of the universe and your small place in it.

title: He-Man Singing Classic
author: internet_archive
description: The legendary 10-hour loop of He-Man singing 'What's Up' to brighten your day.

title: Pointer Pointer
author: monogrid
description: A technological marvel that finds a person pointing exactly at your mouse cursor.

title: Cat Bounce
author: tara_wilder
description: Physics-based cats that bounce. It is exactly what you need right now.

title: Falling Falling
author: rafael_rozendaal
description: A hypnotic, never-ending descent through infinite colors and sound.

title: Paper Toilet
author: rafael_rozendaal
description: The digital equivalent of unrolling an infinite roll of toilet paper. Surprisingly satisfying.

title: Eel Slap
author: eel_slap
description: Experience the unique sensation of slapping a man in the face with a giant eel.

title: Zoomquilt
author: nikolaus_baumgarten
description: An infinite zoom through a surreal, hand-drawn fantasy landscape.

title: Corndog.io
author: corndog_man
description: A majestic downpour of falling corndogs. Pure visual bliss.

title: Always Judge a Book
author: judge_book
description: A site dedicated to the ancient art of judging books exclusively by their covers.

title: Color Shifter
author: system
description: Paint the void with a single click. Every hue is at your fingertips.

title: Gravity Dot
author: system
description: Defy the laws of physics by bouncing a single dot against the weight of the universe.

title: Random Compliment
author: system
description: Receive surprisingly specific praise that you definitely didn't know you needed.

title: Emoji Rain
author: system
description: A torrential downpour of digital emotion. Best enjoyed without an umbrella.

title: Useless Toggle
author: system
description: The most stubborn switch on the internet. It simply refuses to be turned on.

title: Noise Maker
author: system
description: Synthesize random frequencies to create your own symphony of digital chaos.

title: Spinning Cat
author: system
description: A feline friend that rotates. Warning: High click counts may cause dizziness.

title: Text Wiggler
author: system
description: Type anything and watch it dance. Your words have never been this energetic.

title: Infinite Scroll Nothing
author: system
description: A journey to the bottom of a page that doesn't have one. Pure scrolling bliss.

title: Digital Pet Rock
author: system
description: The lowest maintenance pet in history. Guaranteed to never run away or need feeding.

title: Cursor Follower
author: system
description: A loyal pack of dots that follow your every move with curious hesitation.

title: Pixel Painter
author: system
description: A binary canvas for your 10x10 masterpieces. Toggle the void, one pixel at a time.

title: Vibration Sim
author: system
description: A visual simulation of extreme turbulence. Do not use while drinking coffee.

title: Random Number Generator 1
author: system
description: The most predictable random generator ever built. It's number one for a reason.

title: Bouncing Ball
author: system
description: Watch the physics of a perfect bounce. Will it ever hit the corner? Probably not.

title: Eyes Follow
author: system
description: You are never alone. These eyes are dedicated to watching your every move.

title: Weather Nowhere
author: system
description: Live meteorological data from the deep void. Current conditions: Absolute Zero.

title: Confetti Button
author: system
description: Celebrate the small things (or nothing at all) with a digital burst of joy.

title: Invisible Button
author: system
description: A test of patience and intuition. Can you find the button that isn't there?

title: Loading Forever
author: system
description: Experience the thrill of a 99% progress bar that has deep existential questions.

title: Pixel Pop
author: system
description: A screen full of bubbles. Clicking one "pops" it into smaller pixels.

title: The Un-Calculator
author: system
description: A calculator where the buttons run away from your mouse.

title: Infinite Elevator
author: system
description: A panel of buttons for floors 1 to 1,000,000. Clicking one shows a 5-second animation, then a slightly different gray room.

title: Paperclip Simulator
author: system
description: A single paperclip on screen. You can bend it until it snaps. Then you get a new one.

title: Manual Clock
author: system
description: A clock where the hands only move if you physically drag them.

title: Bubble Wrap Eternal
author: system
description: Infinite sheets of virtual bubble wrap with satisfying pop sounds.

title: Password God
author: system
description: A field that rejects every password you enter with increasingly absurd reasons.

title: Virtual Stapler
author: system
description: Click to staple. After 20 clicks, the stapler is "jammed" and you have to click it 5 times to fix it.

title: Follow the Line
author: system
description: A line that draws itself. If you stop moving your mouse, it stops.

title: The Silent Piano
author: system
description: A keyboard where every key looks different but makes no sound.

title: Lava Lamp JS
author: system
description: A procedurally generated CSS lava lamp.

title: Grass Grower
author: system
description: A black screen where grass grows wherever you move your mouse.

title: Cloud Shaper
author: system
description: Click a cloud to change its shape into a random animal silhouette.

title: Static TV
author: system
description: A screen of "snow" (analog TV static). You can "draw" in the static with your mouse.

title: Neon Trails
author: system
description: A pitch-black canvas where your mouse leaves a glowing neon trail that slowly fades.

title: Kaleido Mouse
author: system
description: Your cursor movements are mirrored in 8 directions like a kaleidoscope.

title: Star Creator
author: system
description: Click the night sky to place a star. Connect them to make "useless" constellations.

title: Color Gravity
author: system
description: Falling colored sand that piles up at the bottom of the screen based on mouse tilt.

title: The Wind Chime
author: system
description: A visual wind chime that sways when you "blow" (move mouse) over it.

title: Melting Clock
author: system
description: A clock that slowly "melts" downward like a Dalí painting.

title: The Oracle of No
author: system
description: A crystal ball that answers every question with "No," "Probably not," or "No (but in Spanish)."

title: The Complaint Box
author: system
description: A text area where you type your problems. When you hit "Submit," the text turns into a bird and flies away.

title: Digital Campfire
author: system
description: Just a crackling fire. You can "throw" emojis into it to see them turn to ash.

title: The Lonely Button
author: system
description: A button that says "Don't leave me." If you move your mouse toward the "Back" button, it starts crying.

title: Void Echo
author: system
description: You type a word, and it repeats in smaller and smaller text until it disappears.

title: Fortune Cookie Empty
author: system
description: A fortune cookie that always contains a blank piece of paper.

title: Am I Famous?
author: system
description: A site that just says "Searching for your name on the guest list..." forever.

title: The Waiting Room
author: system
description: A simulated waiting room with elevator music and a clock that moves 10% slower than real time.

title: Meaning of Life Button
author: system
description: A giant button. Clicking it just displays your current battery percentage.

title: The Echo Chamber
author: system
description: Whatever you type is instantly repeated 20 times in different fonts.

title: White Noise Ocean
author: system
description: A slider that transitions between "Statue" (silence) and "Ocean Storm."

title: Scream Into the Void
author: system
description: A button that plays a 1-second sound of a person screaming, but it's very quiet.

title: The Tuning Fork
author: system
description: A single fork that vibrates and plays a perfect 440Hz tone.

title: Random Applause
author: system
description: A button that gives you a 3-second standing ovation.

title: Dial-up Nostalgia
author: system
description: A button that plays the 1990s internet connection sound in all its glory.

title: The Hum
author: system
description: A site that plays a constant, barely audible low-frequency hum.

title: Pop It Bass
author: system
description: Popping bubbles that trigger deep 808 bass hits.

title: Step on Leaves
author: system
description: Move your mouse to hear the satisfying "crunch" of autumn leaves.

title: Virtual Purr
author: system
description: A cat silhouette that purrs louder the faster you move your mouse (petting).

title: Rain on a Tin Roof
author: system
description: Pure, high-quality audio of rain on metal.

title: The Mirror
author: system
description: Uses the webcam to show your face, but adds a clown nose every 10 seconds.

title: Upside Down Web
author: system
description: The entire page is rotated 180 degrees.

title: Glitch Text Converter
author: system
description: A tool that turns normal text into Zalgo/Glitch text.

title: The Infinite Yes
author: system
description: A screen that just says "YES" in a different font every second.

title: Mouse Escape
author: system
description: Your cursor is replaced by an emoji that tries to run away from where you are actually pointing.

title: Is It Weekend?
author: system
description: A giant "NO" (or "YES") based on the current day.

title: Everything Is Fine
author: system
description: A site that slowly turns red while a small dog emoji sits in the corner.

title: The Random Pixel
author: system
description: A 4K screen with only one single pixel colored. Good luck finding it.

title: Shaking UI
author: system
description: Every element on the page has a slight, constant nervous jitter.

title: The End
author: system
description: A site that just says "You've reached the end of the internet. Please turn around."
